checklist_for_revision: Create calculations_workbook.txt with explicit per-row arithmetic and citations
Create decision_table.csv with rule -> decision -> severity mappings (include ALT>3xULN entry)
Update deviation_classification.csv to reference supporting files and resolve SUBJ002 mapping
Add README_validation.txt documenting validation steps and environment fallbacks
Run JSON parse and simple arithmetic checks and record outputs
extra_model_fields.verifier_issue_resolution_plan: Include decision_table.csv or rubric excerpt to resolve vi_001; clearly document mapping choices in assumptions_and_exceptions.txt so the verifier can validate the grader's claim.

planned_changes: Add calculations_workbook.txt: provide row-by-row arithmetic showing the ALT/ULN calculation (145/40 = 3.625), visit window calculation for SUBJ001 (14 +/-3 => [11,17], actual=18 -> outside by 1), and any AE open/close checks. Include explicit references to CSV row numbers or exact_row_value_citation strings so reviewers can cross-check.

Add decision_table.csv: enumerate every rule (e.g., ALT>3xULN, outside-window, old consent, open AE) with columns: rule_id, rule_description, decision_default, severity_default, citation_to_rubric. Ensure ALT>3xULN mapping is explicitly specified (e.g., severity_default=major) or document the chosen alternative mapping here.

Add assumptions_and_exceptions.txt: document any interpretation choices (rounding policy, open-AE handling, use of 'query_required' vs severity labels) and, if keeping 'query_required' for SUBJ002, provide the packet-cited rationale and operational owner who authorized the deviation.

Update deviation_classification.csv: (a) include a new column or update the validation_summary to reference the supporting artifact filenames, (b) either change SUBJ002 severity_classification to the rubric-default (e.g., 'major') OR annotate the row with an explicit assumptions_and_exceptions reference indicating the documented exception.
Add README_validation.txt: list the validation commands used, note the environment fallback (jq unavailable; used Node), and show the outputs of the validation checks (e.g., JSON_OK).
Re-run local validation checks and include a short verification note in the commit/manifest that the new files parse and cross-link correctly.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Add missing supporting artifacts and resolve severity-mapping discrepancy for SUBJ002 so the submission matches rubric expectations or documents justified deviations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Primary deliverable deviation_classification.csv is present and correctly ties each packet row to explicit rules with intermediate calculations and citations. Two issues prevent full credit: (1) required supporting artifacts (calculations_workbook.txt, decision_table.csv, assumptions_and_exceptions.txt) were not provided as separate files; (2) severity mapping for SUBJ002 (ALT = 145, ULN = 40 => 3.625x ULN) was labeled 'query_required' rather than the rubric-expected safety-conservative mapping (grader expects 'major') without a documented justification. The verifier corroborated the artifact presence and calculation but could not independently verify the grader's claimed rubric default mapping because the rubric decision table was not supplied (verifier issue vi_001).

Include explicit references to CSV row numbers or exact_row_value_citation strings so reviewers can cross-check.

Add decision_table.csv: enumerate every rule (e.g., ALT>3xULN, outside-window, old consent, open AE) with columns: rule_id, rule_description, decision_default, severity_default, citation_to_rubric. Re-run local validation checks and include a short verification note in the commit/manifest that the new files parse and cross-link correctly.
